title: adenia gummifera var. gummifera
intro: Adenia gummifera var. gummifera, also known as the African milk tree or wild cucumber, is a plant species in the family Passifloraceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating wounds, burns, and skin infections due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Adenia gummifera var. gummifera include its ability to accelerate wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's latex is often used to make ointments, creams, and tinctures, which are applied topically to affected areas to harness its therapeutic benefits.
